title: Braemar Weekly Dry Market Report
summary:
Main
By Mark Nugent

Looking towards the winter
Energy prices are continuing to firm in Europe and weather is driving supply/demand changes across several regions. We look at how these recent developments shape the outlook for seaborne coal demand going forward. More issues on the way in Australia?

children.title: Looking towards the winter
children.summary: Energy prices are continuing to firm in Europe and weather is driving supply/demand changes across several regions. We look at how these recent developments shape the outlook for seaborne coal demand going forward.

children.title: More issues on the way in Australia?
section_path: More issues on the way in Australia?
children.section_path_text: More issues on the way in Australia?
children.summary: Following the heavily disrupted month of July for Australian coal exports, shipments in August are on track to recover back above 30m tonnes. In July, the extreme weather resulted in the country's lowest level of monthly coal exports on record, amounting to 24.4m tonnes.

children.title: China drought disrupting hydro output
children.summary: In China, several weather stations along the Yangtze have reportedly recorded their highest ever temperatures in the past week. According to Reuters estimates, hydro-to-coal replacement for the rest of the year in China has increased by 16.2m tonnes versus last month's predictions due to the persistent dryness.

children.title: Rhine water levels back above minimum
section_path: Rhine water levels back above minimum
children.section_path_text: Rhine water levels back above minimum
children.summary: Following weeks of falling water levels on the Rhine, which inherently reduced ARA ports' capacity to discharge coal, some relief has come with wetter weather as the river have returned above the minimum level for barges. Subsequently, coal stocks have started to fall at ports in the region.
